item.title: Latest U.S. strike on alleged drug boat kills 2 in Caribbean, military says
item.description: The U.S. military says it launched another strike on a boat accused of ferrying drugs in the Caribbean Sea, killing two people, as number of recent strikes continues to ramp up.

item.title: U.S. sinks 7 Iranian boats as Iran attacks UAE, ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran launched attacks on the UAE and tankers in the Strait of Hormuz while the U.S. destroyed Iranian boats "that attempted to interfere" with Project Freedom.

item.title: Fireworks plant explosion in China kills at least 26, state media say
item.description: An explosion at a fireworks plant in a province in central China killed at least 26 people and injured 61 others, authorities said. The cause was unknown. The person in charge was detained.

item.title: See the best looks of the Met Gala 2026
item.description: Rihanna, Sabrina Carpenter, Madonna, and Beyoncé all wore their best looks to celebrate the "Costume Art" theme at the 2026 Met Gala. Here are some of the best moments, photos, and more from the night.

item.title: Best and most unforgettable 2026 Met Gala looks
item.description: Celebrities graced the Met's green and white carpet with their interpretations of this year's dress code, "Fashion is Art," inspired by the exhibit "Costume Art." Here are some of the most unforgettable, showstopping looks of the night.

item.title: Breaking down the gerrymandering battle across the U.S.
item.description: Florida has become the latest state to approve a partisan gerrymandered congressional map. CBS News executive director of elections and surveys Anthony Salvanto has a look at the nationwide congressional redistricting moves.

item.title: 5/4: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Fallout continues from Spirit Airlines' closure; new details on missing U.S. soldiers in Morocco.

item.title: 5/4: CBS Evening News
item.description: Secret Service shoots gunman near Washington Monument; United flight was only 20 feet above New Jersey Turnpike when landing gear hit truck.

item.title: Judge expresses concern over treatment of correspondents' dinner shooting suspect
item.description: A federal judge on Monday criticized the treatment of accused White House Correspondents' Dinner attacker Cole Allen, at one point apologizing to the defendant for what he saw as overly restrictive and punitive conditions at the D.C. jail where he is being held. CBS News' Jake Rosen reports.

item.title: SCOTUS lets redistricting ruling take effect immediately, sparking angry exchange
item.description: The Supreme Court on Monday allowed last week's landmark decision striking down Louisiana's congressional map to take effect immediately, drawing a sharp back-and-forth between two justices.

item.title: Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni reach settlement in "It Ends With Us" lawsuit
item.description: A trial in the lawsuit between Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni was set to begin later in May.

item.title: Celebrities arrive at Met Gala amid backlash over tech billionaires' presence
item.description: The Met Gala got underway in New York City Monday night, but it wasn't without controversy. CBS News reporter Jared Ochacher has more.

item.title: The risks of Trump's plan to guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. has launched President Trump's new "Project Freedom" operation to guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News national security contributor Samantha Vinograd has more.

item.title: 3 dead in suspected hantavirus outbreak on cruise ship
item.description: An apparent hantavirus outbreak has killed three people on a cruise ship in the Atlantic Ocean. CBS News' Tom Hanson and Dr. Céline Gounder have more.

item.title: Passenger stranded by Spirit Airlines' closure on her experience: "We found another way"
item.description: Lyndee Domka was in Orlando, Florida, when her flight home to Ann Arbor, Michigan, was canceled due to the closure of Spirit Airlines. She joined CBS News to discuss her experience.

item.title: White House grappling with bad poll numbers on Iran war, economy ahead of midterms
item.description: The Trump administration is facing terrible approval ratings ahead of the midterm elections, according to new polling from ABC News and the Washington Post. CBS News' Kathryn Watson and Eleanor Watson have more.

item.title: Ben's Chili Bowl reopens in Washington, D.C.
item.description: Major Garrett reports on the reopening of the iconic Ben's Chili Bowl in the nation's capital.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: Babe Ruth interviewed
item.description: A 1939 interview with Babe Ruth about autographs is the subject of this CBS News Radio flashback, introduced by Major Garrett.

item.title: 1 missing U.S. soldier in Morocco was trying to save another, officials say
item.description: Search and rescue efforts were still underway as of Monday after the two U.S. Army soldiers went missing off Morocco's southern coast over the weekend while off duty during a training exercise.

item.title: How math could solve gerrymandering
item.description: Roland Fryer, economics professor at Harvard University, joins CBS News to discuss how math might hold the key to ending gerrymandering.

item.title: 2 U.S. Navy destroyers transit Strait of Hormuz after dodging Iranian onslaught
item.description: Two U.S. Navy destroyers transited the Strait of Hormuz and entered the Persian Gulf after navigating an Iranian barrage, according to defense officials.

item.title: Trump's disapproval rating reaches all-time high in new poll
item.description: A new poll from ABC News and the Washington Post shows President Trump's disapproval rating at 62%, an all-time high. Political strategists Tiffany Smiley and Ameshia Cross joined CBS News to discuss.

item.title: Vehicle carrying explosives crashes into Portland athletic club, driver dead
item.description: A vehicle carrying explosives crashed through the front entrance of an athletic club in downtown Portland, Oregon. The driver was killed.

item.title: Alito temporarily restores FDA rule allowing abortion pill to be sent by mail
item.description: A federal appeals court blocked a FDA rule that allowed the abortion pill mifepristone to be dispensed through the mail.

item.title: United flight was only about 20 feet above New Jersey Turnpike when landing gear hit truck
item.description: The landing gear of a United Airlines flight hit a truck on the New Jersey Turnpike Sunday while it was landing at Newark Airport. Kris Van Cleave has the latest.

item.title: Shooting at lake near Oklahoma City leaves at least 18 injured
item.description: At least 18 people were at hospitals following a shooting at a party at Arcadia Lake, near Oklahoma City, police say.

item.title: Judge "very troubled" by accused correspondents' dinner gunman's treatment in jail
item.description: A federal judge on Monday sharply criticized the treatment of accused White House Correspondents' Dinner attacker Cole Allen, at one point apologizing to the defendant for what he saw as overly restrictive and punitive conditions in jail.

item.title: Press gala shooting suspect moved off suicide watch, records show
item.description: In a motion requesting Allen's removal from suicide watch, his lawyers said that the restrictions amount to "violations of his rights under the Due Process Clause."

item.title: First glimpse of 2028 Democratic field at National Action Network Convention
item.description: A horde of 2028 Democratic presidential hopefuls attended Al Sharpton's National Action Network conference, nearly two years before the first primary votes will be cast.

item.title: Character AI pushes dangerous content to kids, parents and researchers say | 60 Minutes
item.description: A teen told a Character AI chatbot 55 times that she was feeling suicidal. Her parents say the chatbot never provided resources for her to get help. They are one of at least six families suing the company.
